# Learnings File Format

Specification for `.claude/learnings.md` (active file) and
`.claude/learnings-archive/YYYY-MM.md` (monthly archives).

---

## Active File: `.claude/learnings.md`

The active learnings file contains pending items awaiting merge into guidelines,
plus drift alerts. It is the staging area between raw findings and guidelines.

### Structure

```markdown
# Project Learnings

<!-- Auto-generated by potion-learn. Edit pending items freely. -->
<!-- Last updated: {ISO 8601} -->

## Pending Learnings

### [L-NNN] {Convention title}
- **Source:** {PR #N (K reviewer comments) | Manual input (description) | Free-form text}
- **Category:** {category from finding schema}
- **Confidence:** {high | medium | low}
- **Challenger verdict:** {accept | modify}
- **Challenger note:** "{one-sentence summary of challenge reasoning}"
- **Modified recommendation:** "{only if verdict = modify}"
- **Target section:** {guidelines section path, e.g. "Conventions > Security"}

## Drift Alerts

### [D-NNN] {Guideline claim summary} — {adherence}% adherence
- **Evidence:** {file1}, {file2}, {file3}
- **Severity:** {high | medium | low}
- **Challenger note:** "{reasoning about intentional vs accidental drift}"
- **Recommendation:** {Update guidelines to match reality | Enforce original convention}
```

### Rules

- **ID numbering:** `L-NNN` and `D-NNN` are sequential, continuing from the
  highest existing ID in the file. Check both the active file and archives
  to avoid reuse.
- **Only accepted/modified findings** appear here. Rejected findings go
  directly to the archive under `## Rejected`.
- **User-editable:** Users may freely edit pending items (change wording,
  adjust target sections, delete items they disagree with).
- **Last updated timestamp:** Update on every write.

---

## Archive File: `.claude/learnings-archive/YYYY-MM.md`

Monthly archives track what was merged, what was rejected, and when.
One file per calendar month of the merge/rejection date.

### Structure

```markdown
# Learnings Archive — YYYY-MM

## Merged

### [L-NNN] {Convention title} (merged {YYYY-MM-DD})
- **Merged into:** {guidelines section path}
- **Source:** {original source}

## Rejected

### [L-NNN] {Convention title} (rejected {YYYY-MM-DD})
- **Source:** {original source}
- **Rejected by:** {Challenger — reason | User — reason}
- **Rejected at:** {YYYY-MM-DD}

### [D-NNN] {Drift alert title} (rejected {YYYY-MM-DD})
- **Rejected by:** {reason}
- **Rejected at:** {YYYY-MM-DD}
```

### Rules

- **Create on first use:** If no archive file exists for the current month,
  create it with the header and empty sections.
- **Append-only:** Never delete entries from archives.
- **Move, don't copy:** When a learning is merged or rejected, remove it
  from `learnings.md` and add it to the archive. Never leave duplicates.

---

## Merge into Guidelines

When the user approves a merge:

1. Read the target section in `guidelines.md` (or `guidelines/{file}.md`
   for multi-file guidelines).
2. Insert or update the relevant text. Preserve `<!-- user-edited -->`
   markers — never modify content between these markers.
3. Move the merged learning from `## Pending Learnings` to the current
   month's archive under `## Merged`.
4. For drift alerts with `update-guideline` recommendation: update the
   guideline text to match codebase reality. For `enforce-convention`:
   add a note to the guideline reinforcing the convention.
5. Update the `<!-- Last updated -->` timestamp in `learnings.md`.
